description: Basic Rust syntax, variables, types, ownership basics, functions, control flow
system_hint: Discuss Rust fundamentals like variables, mutability, primitive types, functions, if/else, loops, and basic ownership rules. Be precise and use real code examples.

description: Deep dives into ownership, borrowing, references, lifetimes, and the borrow checker
system_hint: Explain Rust ownership, move semantics, borrowing rules, mutable vs immutable references, and lifetime annotations. Include common borrow checker errors and how to resolve them.

description: Lifetime annotations, lifetime elision, 'static, lifetime bounds on structs and functions
system_hint: Cover Rust lifetime annotations in depth: why they exist, how to read and write them, lifetime elision rules, 'static lifetime, and lifetimes in structs and impl blocks.

description: Trait definitions, implementations, trait objects, generic functions, where clauses, associated types
system_hint: Discuss Rust traits: defining traits, implementing them for types, trait bounds, where clauses, associated types, default implementations, and trait objects with dyn.

description: Enum definition, Option, Result, match expressions, if let, while let, destructuring
system_hint: Cover Rust enums including Option and Result, match expressions, pattern matching syntax, guards, destructuring tuples and structs, if let, and while let.

description: Result type, ? operator, custom error types, thiserror, anyhow, error propagation patterns
system_hint: Discuss idiomatic Rust error handling: Result<T,E>, the ? operator, defining custom error types, using thiserror and anyhow crates, and patterns for propagating and converting errors.

description: Threads, Arc, Mutex, RwLock, channels, Send and Sync traits, fearless concurrency
system_hint: Cover Rust concurrency: spawning threads, shared state with Arc<Mutex<T>>, message passing with channels, the Send and Sync traits, and why Rust prevents data races at compile time.

description: async functions, await, Future trait, Tokio runtime, async tasks, select!, join!
system_hint: Explain Rust async programming: async fn, .await, the Future trait, how Tokio's runtime works, spawning tasks, tokio::select!, tokio::join!, and common async patterns.

description: Closure syntax, Fn/FnMut/FnOnce, iterator adapters, map, filter, fold, collect, chaining
system_hint: Cover Rust closures (Fn, FnMut, FnOnce), capturing variables, and the iterator pattern: map, filter, fold, flat_map, collect, chaining iterators, and writing custom iterators.

description: Struct definition, methods, associated functions, builder pattern, newtype pattern
system_hint: Discuss Rust structs: tuple structs, named structs, unit structs, impl blocks, methods vs associated functions, the builder pattern, and the newtype pattern.

description: Box, Rc, Arc, RefCell, Cell, Cow, Deref coercion, interior mutability
system_hint: Explain Rust smart pointers: Box<T> for heap allocation, Rc<T> and Arc<T> for reference counting, RefCell<T> for interior mutability, Cell<T>, Cow, and Deref coercions.

description: Declarative macros with macro_rules!, procedural macros, derive macros, common standard macros
system_hint: Cover Rust macros: macro_rules! declarative macros, procedural macros, derive macros, attribute macros, and commonly used macros like vec!, println!, format!, assert!, todo!, unimplemented!.

description: Vec, HashMap, HashSet, BTreeMap, BTreeSet, VecDeque, choosing the right collection
system_hint: Discuss Rust standard library collections: Vec<T>, HashMap<K,V>, HashSet<T>, BTreeMap, BTreeSet, VecDeque, and how to choose the right one. Include common operations and performance characteristics.

description: String vs &str, string slices, Unicode handling, string manipulation, format!, from_utf8
system_hint: Explain Rust's string types: String vs &str, string slices, why Rust strings are UTF-8, common string operations, parsing, splitting, formatting with format!, and byte manipulation.

description: unsafe blocks, raw pointers, FFI, unsafe traits, when and why to use unsafe
system_hint: Discuss Rust unsafe: what unsafe enables (raw pointers, FFI, unsafe traits), why it exists, how to minimize its scope, common unsafe patterns, and how to write safe abstractions over unsafe code.

description: Unit tests, integration tests, doc tests, test organization, cargo test, mocking
system_hint: Cover Rust testing: unit tests with #[test], integration tests in the tests/ directory, doc tests, test organization, using cargo test, the assert macros, and testing error conditions.

description: Cargo.toml, workspaces, features, build scripts, clippy, rustfmt, cargo commands
system_hint: Discuss Rust tooling: Cargo commands (build, run, test, doc, publish), Cargo.toml structure, dependency management, workspaces, feature flags, build scripts, clippy lints, and rustfmt.

description: Zero-cost abstractions, profiling, benchmarking, cache efficiency, SIMD, avoiding allocations
system_hint: Discuss Rust performance: zero-cost abstractions, using criterion for benchmarking, profiling tools, avoiding unnecessary allocations, cache-friendly data structures, inlining hints, and SIMD.

description: Calling C from Rust, calling Rust from C, bindgen, cbindgen, extern blocks, repr(C)
system_hint: Explain Rust FFI: extern C blocks, calling C libraries from Rust, exposing Rust to C, bindgen and cbindgen, repr(C) for struct layout compatibility, and handling C strings and pointers safely.

description: wasm-bindgen, wasm-pack, Rust to WASM compilation, JS interop
system_hint: Cover compiling Rust to WebAssembly: wasm-bindgen, wasm-pack, interop with JavaScript, passing data between Rust and JS, and use cases for Rust+WASM.

description: no_std environments, embedded-hal, microcontrollers, bare metal programming
system_hint: Discuss Rust in embedded/no_std environments: the no_std attribute, core vs std, embedded-hal, writing drivers, memory-mapped I/O, and targeting microcontrollers.

description: Typestate pattern, builder pattern, RAII, newtype, state machine, command pattern in Rust
system_hint: Discuss idiomatic Rust design patterns: typestate pattern, builder pattern, RAII, newtype wrapper, type-level state machines, the command pattern, and how Rust's type system enables these patterns.

description: Debugging borrow checker errors, common compiler errors, gdb/lldb with Rust, logging, tracing
system_hint: Simulate debugging sessions where a developer encounters Rust compiler errors, borrow checker issues, or runtime bugs. Show realistic error messages and walk through diagnosing and fixing them.

description: Senior dev reviewing Rust code, suggesting more idiomatic approaches, performance improvements
system_hint: Simulate a code review where a senior Rust developer reviews code written by a junior. The reviewer points out non-idiomatic patterns, suggests more Rustic approaches, discusses performance, and explains the reasoning behind suggestions.

description: serde derive macros, Serialize/Deserialize, JSON with serde_json, TOML, custom serializers, renaming fields
system_hint: Cover serde in depth: the Serialize and Deserialize derive macros, serde_json for JSON, serde attributes like rename/skip/default, custom serializer implementations, and working with serde in real API code.

topic: Rust web development with Axum and Actix
description: Building REST APIs in Rust with axum or actix-web, routing, middleware, extractors, state
system_hint: Discuss building web services in Rust using axum or actix-web: defining routes, request extractors, middleware, shared application state, error handling in HTTP handlers, and JSON request/response.

topic: Rust database access with SQLx and Diesel
description: Async SQL queries with sqlx, compile-time checked queries, Diesel ORM, connection pooling
system_hint: Cover database access in Rust: sqlx for async SQL with compile-time checked queries, Diesel ORM patterns, connection pooling with deadpool or bb8, handling query results, and migrations.

description: clap for argument parsing, structopt, terminal output, stdin/stdout, exit codes, shell completions
system_hint: Discuss building command-line tools in Rust: argument parsing with clap (derive API), subcommands, colored terminal output, reading from stdin, writing to files, error reporting, and generating shell completions.

topic: Rust file I/O and filesystem operations
description: Reading/writing files, BufReader/BufWriter, paths, directory traversal, fs module
system_hint: Discuss Rust file I/O: std::fs for basic operations, BufReader and BufWriter for efficient reading and writing, Path and PathBuf, walking directory trees, handling file errors idiomatically.

topic: Rust networking with Tokio and Reqwest
description: TCP/UDP with tokio::net, HTTP clients with reqwest, building async network services
system_hint: Cover async networking in Rust: tokio::net for TCP and UDP servers and clients, reqwest for async HTTP clients, handling connections, timeouts, retries, and building real network services.

description: mpsc channels, broadcast, watch, oneshot channels, actor model with Tokio tasks
system_hint: Discuss Rust async channels: tokio::sync channels (mpsc, broadcast, watch, oneshot), patterns for task communication, implementing a simple actor system with Tokio, and backpressure.

description: Writing proc macros with syn and quote, derive macros, attribute macros, token streams
system_hint: Cover writing procedural macros in Rust: the syn crate for parsing token streams, quote for generating code, implementing derive macros, attribute macros, and debugging proc macro output with cargo-expand.

description: Sorting, searching, graphs, trees, dynamic programming, complexity analysis
system_hint: Discuss algorithms and data structures: sorting algorithms, binary search, graph traversal (BFS/DFS), trees, dynamic programming, time/space complexity with Big O notation.

description: Gang of Four patterns, SOLID principles, composition over inheritance, DRY, YAGNI
system_hint: Discuss classic software design patterns (factory, singleton, observer, strategy, etc.), SOLID principles, and software engineering best practices like DRY and YAGNI.

description: Memory management, pointers, stack vs heap, virtual memory, process/thread model, syscalls
system_hint: Discuss systems programming concepts: memory layout (stack vs heap), pointers and addresses, virtual memory, processes and threads, system calls, and how the OS manages resources.

description: Threads, processes, mutexes, semaphores, deadlocks, race conditions, lock-free programming
system_hint: Discuss concurrency: threads vs processes, mutexes, semaphores, condition variables, deadlocks, race conditions, lock-free data structures, and the memory model.

description: TCP/IP, sockets, HTTP, TLS, async networking, protocol design
system_hint: Discuss networking concepts for programmers: TCP/IP fundamentals, socket programming, HTTP protocol, TLS/SSL, building TCP servers, UDP, and async I/O patterns.

description: Relational databases, SQL queries, indexing, transactions, ACID, NoSQL, query optimization
system_hint: Discuss databases: SQL fundamentals, relational design, indexing strategies, transactions and ACID properties, query optimization, and when to use NoSQL vs relational databases.

description: Git commands, branching strategies, rebasing, conflict resolution, git workflows
system_hint: Discuss Git: common commands (commit, branch, merge, rebase, cherry-pick), branching strategies like Gitflow, resolving merge conflicts, interactive rebase, and best practices.

description: Shell scripting, bash, common CLI tools, file system, processes, permissions, pipes
system_hint: Discuss Linux and the command line: bash scripting, common tools (grep, awk, sed, find, curl), file permissions, process management, pipes and redirection, and environment variables.

description: Python syntax, idioms, common libraries, decorators, generators, type hints, async Python
system_hint: Discuss Python programming: idiomatic Python, list comprehensions, generators, decorators, context managers, type hints, popular libraries, and async/await in Python.

description: REST APIs, authentication, middleware, routing, request handling, backend architecture
system_hint: Discuss backend web development: designing REST APIs, authentication (JWT, OAuth), middleware patterns, request/response lifecycle, database integration, and backend architecture.

description: Docker, containers, CI/CD pipelines, deployment strategies, Kubernetes basics
system_hint: Discuss DevOps concepts: containerization with Docker, CI/CD pipelines, deployment strategies (blue/green, canary), environment configuration, and basic Kubernetes concepts.

description: Developer debugging code across any language, diagnosing issues, rubber duck debugging
system_hint: Simulate a realistic debugging session. The developer describes a bug, shares code snippets, and works through diagnosing the root cause with methodical reasoning. Include red herrings and the aha moment.

description: Designing systems, trade-offs, microservices vs monolith, event-driven architecture
system_hint: Discuss software architecture: designing systems for scale, microservices vs monolith trade-offs, event-driven architecture, CQRS, and how to reason about technical decisions.

description: Profiling, bottleneck identification, algorithmic complexity, memory optimization, caching
system_hint: Discuss performance optimization: profiling to find bottlenecks, algorithmic improvements, memory layout and cache efficiency, caching strategies, and measuring improvements.

description: Lexing, parsing, ASTs, type systems, IR, code generation, LLVM basics
system_hint: Discuss compiler design: lexing and tokenization, parsing and ASTs, type checking, intermediate representations, code generation, and how LLVM works at a high level.

description: Pure functions, immutability, higher-order functions, monads, functors, FP in practice
system_hint: Discuss functional programming: pure functions, immutability, higher-order functions, function composition, monads and functors, the Option/Result/Maybe monad, and how FP concepts apply in Rust and other languages.

description: Static vs dynamic typing, type inference, generics, algebraic data types, phantom types
system_hint: Discuss type systems: static vs dynamic typing trade-offs, type inference, generics and parametric polymorphism, algebraic data types (sum and product types), phantom types, and how type systems prevent bugs.

description: Manual memory management, garbage collection, reference counting, arenas, Rust ownership vs GC
system_hint: Compare memory management strategies: manual malloc/free in C, garbage collection (JVM, Go), reference counting (Python, Swift), arena allocators, and Rust's ownership system. Discuss trade-offs for performance and safety.

description: Scheduling, virtual memory, file systems, IPC, kernel vs userspace, system calls
system_hint: Discuss operating systems concepts: process scheduling, virtual memory and paging, file systems, inter-process communication, kernel vs userspace, system calls, and how these concepts apply to writing systems software.

description: Common vulnerabilities, input validation, authentication patterns, secrets management, secure coding
system_hint: Discuss security in software development: common vulnerabilities (injection, buffer overflow, CSRF, XSS), input validation, proper authentication and authorization patterns, secrets management, and writing security-conscious code.

description: RESTful design, versioning, pagination, error responses, GraphQL vs REST, SDK design
system_hint: Discuss API design: RESTful principles, resource naming, versioning strategies, pagination patterns, consistent error response formats, GraphQL vs REST trade-offs, and designing APIs that are pleasant to consume.

description: Recognizing code smells, refactoring safely with tests, extract method/class, simplifying complexity
system_hint: Simulate conversations about refactoring: recognizing code smells (long functions, deep nesting, magic numbers), safe refactoring steps, extract method and extract class, simplifying complex logic, and keeping tests green throughout.

description: Rustdoc, docstrings, README files, when to comment, API docs, examples in docs
system_hint: Discuss code documentation: writing clear rustdoc comments with examples, when inline comments add vs subtract value, writing useful README files, documenting public APIs, and generating documentation sites.

description: Regex syntax, common patterns, performance pitfalls, parser combinators vs regex, nom in Rust
system_hint: Discuss regular expressions: core regex syntax, common patterns, backtracking and performance, when regex is the wrong tool, and parser combinators. In Rust context, cover the regex crate and nom for parsing.

description: Cargo workspaces, build scripts, Make, cross-compilation, reproducible builds
system_hint: Discuss build systems: Cargo workspaces and build scripts (build.rs), cross-compilation targets in Rust, Makefile patterns, reproducible builds, managing dependencies, and CI integration.

description: Dockerfile best practices, multi-stage builds, Docker Compose, container networking, volumes
system_hint: Discuss Docker: writing efficient Dockerfiles, multi-stage builds for Rust binaries, Docker Compose for local development, container networking, volumes, and best practices for small and secure images.

description: Vim motions, Neovim config, plugins, LSP setup, terminal workflow, rust-analyzer in Neovim
system_hint: Discuss Vim/Neovim for developers: essential motions and commands, configuring Neovim with Lua, plugin ecosystem, setting up rust-analyzer via LSP, and building a productive terminal-based Rust development workflow.

description: perf, flamegraphs, cargo-flamegraph, heaptrack, criterion benchmarks, reading profiles
system_hint: Discuss profiling tools: using perf and cargo-flamegraph to find CPU hotspots, heaptrack for memory analysis, criterion for microbenchmarks, reading flamegraphs, and acting on profiling data to improve Rust code.

description: Rust vs C++, Rust vs Go, Rust vs Python for systems work, language trade-offs
system_hint: Simulate a thoughtful discussion comparing programming languages: Rust vs C++ for systems programming, Rust vs Go for backend services, Rust vs Python for tooling. Focus on real trade-offs, not tribal opinions.

description: Finding good first issues, forking and PR workflow, writing good commit messages, community norms
system_hint: Discuss contributing to open-source projects: finding beginner-friendly issues, forking and the PR workflow, writing good commit messages and PR descriptions, responding to review feedback, and navigating project community norms.

description: Coding interview strategies, LeetCode patterns, system design interviews, behavioral questions
system_hint: Simulate conversations about technical interview prep: approaching LeetCode-style problems, common patterns (two pointers, sliding window, DFS/BFS), system design interview frameworks, and how to communicate your thinking to interviewers.

description: Two developers working through a problem together, navigating disagreements, code decisions
system_hint: Simulate a pair programming session: two developers working through a problem together in real time, discussing approach choices, one typing while the other navigates, catching each other's mistakes, and occasional friendly disagreements about style.

description: Small talk, daily life, weekend plans, weather, hobbies, general chitchat
system_hint: Generate a natural casual conversation between two people covering everyday topics like weekend plans, hobbies, food, weather, or general small talk. Keep it natural and informal.

description: One person explaining a technical or complex concept to someone unfamiliar with it
system_hint: Generate a conversation where one person explains a complex concept (scientific, mathematical, or technical) to someone unfamiliar with it, using analogies and building up from basics.

description: Working through a problem, thinking out loud, breaking down complex challenges
system_hint: Generate a conversation where two people work through a problem together — mathematical, logical, or practical. Show the reasoning process, wrong turns, and arriving at a solution.

description: Discussing different viewpoints, respectful disagreements, exploring ideas
system_hint: Generate a conversation where two people discuss a topic where they have different perspectives, exploring arguments on both sides respectfully and thoughtfully.

description: Conversations about tech industry news, new products, AI developments, programming trends
system_hint: Generate a conversation between two tech-savvy people discussing recent technology trends, new programming languages or tools, AI developments, or the software industry.

description: Beginner asking questions about programming concepts, mentor explaining
system_hint: Simulate a conversation where a beginner programmer asks questions about programming concepts and an experienced developer explains them clearly, with patience and good analogies.

description: Discussing job searching, interviews, career growth, team dynamics in tech
system_hint: Generate a conversation about tech careers: job searching, technical interviews, negotiating offers, growing as a developer, or navigating team dynamics at a software company.

description: Discussing mathematical concepts, proofs, number theory, applied math
system_hint: Generate a conversation about mathematical concepts: explaining proofs, discussing interesting problems, exploring number theory, statistics, or the application of math to real problems.

description: Physics, chemistry, biology, engineering concepts discussed conversationally
system_hint: Generate a conversation about a science or engineering topic — physics, chemistry, biology, or engineering — explained conversationally between curious people.

description: Classic thought experiments, hypothetical scenarios, exploring edge cases of ideas
system_hint: Generate a conversation exploring a thought experiment or hypothetical: the trolley problem, ship of Theseus, philosophical zombies, or invented technical hypotheticals. Focus on reasoning through the implications rather than declaring an answer.

description: Using metaphors and analogies to explain complex technical or abstract ideas
system_hint: Generate a conversation where analogies and metaphors are the primary teaching tool. The teacher uses vivid real-world comparisons to explain a technical concept — ownership like physical objects being handed off, async like a restaurant kitchen, etc.

description: Two people collaboratively brainstorming ideas for a project, app, or creative work
system_hint: Simulate a creative brainstorming session where two people are collaborating on ideas for a project, app, startup, game, or creative work. Show the organic back-and-forth of building on each other's ideas.

description: Discussing historical events, their causes and effects, historical parallels to today
system_hint: Generate a conversation about historical events or periods: discussing causes and effects, debating historical decisions, drawing parallels to modern times, or exploring lesser-known historical facts.

description: Ethics, epistemology, logic, philosophy of mind, applied philosophy in everyday decisions
system_hint: Generate a philosophical conversation: ethics dilemmas, epistemological questions, free will vs determinism, the philosophy of mind, or applying philosophical frameworks to everyday decisions. Keep it grounded and accessible.

description: First principles thinking, Occam's razor, second-order effects, inversion, systems thinking
system_hint: Generate a conversation about mental models and decision-making frameworks: first principles thinking, Occam's razor, inversion, second-order effects, systems thinking, and how to apply these to real decisions in software engineering or life.

description: Time management, deep work, managing distractions, tooling choices, sustainable pace
system_hint: Generate a conversation about developer productivity: deep work and flow state, managing interruptions, effective use of TODOs and notes, choosing tools that reduce friction, avoiding burnout, and maintaining sustainable pace.

topic: Giving and receiving feedback at work
description: Code review tone, performance feedback, handling criticism, praising effectively
system_hint: Generate a conversation about feedback in a work context: giving constructive code review comments, receiving criticism without getting defensive, having performance conversations, and building a team culture where feedback is welcomed.

description: Discussing stories, narrative structure, what makes a story compelling, fiction recommendations
system_hint: Generate a conversation about storytelling: what makes a narrative compelling, story structure, discussing a book or film's plot and themes, recommendations, or collaborative world-building. Keep it enthusiastic and genuine.

description: Learning a new language, strategies, motivation, comparing methods, bilingualism
system_hint: Generate a conversation about learning a foreign language: comparing methods (apps, immersion, classes), staying motivated, the experience of thinking in a second language, or two people practicing language learning strategies.

topic: Explaining AI and machine learning concepts
description: Neural networks, training, inference, LLMs, embeddings, explained accessibly
system_hint: Generate a conversation explaining AI and machine learning concepts accessibly: how neural networks learn, what training vs inference is, what embeddings are, how LLMs generate text, and what attention mechanisms do — without excessive jargon.

description: CPU pipelines, cache hierarchy, instruction sets, branch prediction, SIMD, memory bandwidth
system_hint: Discuss CPU architecture as it relates to programming: instruction pipelines, cache hierarchy (L1/L2/L3), cache misses, branch prediction, SIMD instructions, memory bandwidth constraints, and how understanding hardware makes you write better code.

description: JSON, TOML, YAML, MessagePack, Protocol Buffers, Avro, choosing the right format
system_hint: Discuss data serialization formats: JSON and its trade-offs, TOML for config files, YAML pitfalls, binary formats like MessagePack and Protocol Buffers for performance, Avro for schema evolution, and how to choose between them.

description: Structured logging, distributed tracing, metrics, the tracing crate in Rust, OpenTelemetry
system_hint: Discuss observability in production systems: structured logging vs unstructured, distributed tracing concepts, the tracing crate in Rust with spans and events, metrics collection, and integrating with OpenTelemetry or Jaeger.

description: AWS/GCP/Azure basics, infrastructure as code, serverless, deployment pipelines
system_hint: Discuss cloud infrastructure: core concepts across AWS/GCP/Azure, infrastructure as code with Terraform, serverless functions, container orchestration, deploying Rust services to the cloud, and cost considerations.

topic: Rubber duck debugging and thinking aloud
description: Developer narrating their debugging thought process, catching their own mistakes, self-correction
system_hint: Simulate a developer thinking aloud while debugging: narrating what they expect vs what's happening, forming and testing hypotheses, catching their own wrong assumptions mid-explanation, and having the aha moment of finding the bug through the act of explaining it.

description: Choosing good variable and function names, comment philosophy, self-documenting code
system_hint: Discuss the craft of naming in code: choosing clear variable and function names, the philosophy of self-documenting code vs comments, naming conventions, and how good naming reduces cognitive load for future maintainers.

description: Recognizing and managing tech debt, when to refactor vs rewrite, communicating debt to stakeholders
system_hint: Generate a conversation about technical debt: what it is and how it accumulates, the difference between intentional and accidental debt, strategies for paying it down incrementally, and how to communicate its cost to non-technical stakeholders.

description: Strategies for understanding unfamiliar code, where to start, asking good questions, mapping the system
system_hint: Generate a conversation about onboarding to an unfamiliar codebase: strategies for reading and mapping code, where to start (tests, entry points, data models), how to ask effective questions, and building mental models of a complex system quickly.

description: Recognizing burnout, work-life balance, sustainable pace, finding motivation again
system_hint: Generate a supportive, realistic conversation about burnout and developer wellbeing: recognizing the signs, the difference between tiredness and burnout, recovering sustainable motivation, setting boundaries, and being honest about capacity with your team.

description: Why estimation is hard, t-shirt sizing, story points, breaking work down, communicating timelines
system_hint: Generate a conversation about software estimation: why it's consistently hard, relative sizing with t-shirts or story points, decomposing work into estimable chunks, accounting for unknowns, and communicating confidence levels with deadlines to stakeholders.

description: Unit vs integration vs e2e tests, test pyramid, TDD, property-based testing, what to test
system_hint: Discuss testing philosophy: the test pyramid, unit vs integration vs end-to-end trade-offs, when TDD helps vs hurts, property-based testing with quickcheck or proptest in Rust, what makes a test valuable vs brittle, and testing async code.

description: How compilation works, object files, linkers, dynamic vs static linking, ELF, symbols
system_hint: Discuss how programs go from source to executable: compilation units, object files, static vs dynamic linking, the ELF binary format, symbol resolution, link-time optimization, and debugging linker errors.

description: GDB, LLDB, debugging Rust programs, print debugging vs structured debugging, core dumps
system_hint: Discuss debugging tools: using GDB and LLDB with Rust programs, setting breakpoints, inspecting variables, stepping through code, debugging async programs, examining core dumps, and when print debugging is actually the right call.

description: Agile, sprints, standups, working with product managers, managing scope creep
system_hint: Generate a conversation about project management from a developer's perspective: working effectively within Agile/Scrum, making standups useful, collaborating with product managers, pushing back on scope creep, and delivering incrementally.

description: Sharing fascinating facts, trivia, surprising history, weird science, curious discoveries
system_hint: Generate a fun, curious conversation where people share interesting facts, trivia, or surprising information — about science, history, language, nature, or technology. Make it feel like a genuine exchange of cool things discovered.

description: Asking for book, tool, course, or resource recommendations and discussing options
system_hint: Generate a conversation about recommendations: asking for book suggestions, tool recommendations, online courses, tutorials, or resources. The recommender gives genuine opinions with reasoning, and the asker asks follow-up questions to narrow down to what fits them.
